conversations.content: Today we studied the Thunder Bluff Sacred Hunting Grounds and Ecological Protection Decree. If someone is found engaging in destructive hunting in sacred grounds like Red Cloud Mesa...
Destroying the hunting grounds in Thunder Bluff's sacred lands? The tauren won't forgive you! What happens when caught?
then the Sunwalker guards must immediately stop the hunting activity and confiscate all catches.
Sunwalker guards stop and confiscate — satisfying! Then penalties based on the severity of damage, right?

Then further action is determined based on the situation: if overhunting leads to local ecological imbalance, order the offender to repair the hunting grounds under druid supervision and impose a one-year hunting ban.
Overhunting leads to ecological repair plus one-year ban — a warning. What about using destructive spells like explosives?
If using destructive spells such as elemental explosives to kill creatures, deprive the offender of the right to communicate with Earth Spirits and sentence them to hard labor.
Depriving the right to communicate with Earth Spirits? For a tauren, that's worse than death! The worst is being instigated by the Grimtotem for blood sacrifice hunting, right?

If instigated by the Grimtotem to carry out cruel blood sacrifice hunting, it is judged as the crime of blasphemy against life — banish the mastermind from Mulgore and permanently strip them of their Horde citizenship.

think: Thunder Bluff sacred hunting grounds: destructive hunting found → Sunwalker guards stop + confiscate catches → three outcomes: overhunting ecological imbalance = druid-supervised repair + one-year ban; destructive spell hunting = deprive Earth Spirit communication + hard labor; Grimtotem-instigated blood sacrifice = blasphemy against life, banish from Mulgore + strip Horde citizenship.
memory: Thunder Bluff Sacred Hunting Grounds and Ecological Protection Decree: If someone is found engaging in destructive hunting in sacred grounds like Red Cloud Mesa, then the Sunwalker guards must immediately stop the hunting activity and confiscate all catches, then further action is determined based on the situation: if overhunting leads to local ecological imbalance, order the offender to repair the hunting grounds under druid supervision and impose a one-year hunting ban; if using destructive spells such as elemental explosives to kill creatures, deprive the offender of the right to communicate with Earth Spirits and sentence them to hard labor; if instigated by the Grimtotem to carry out cruel blood sacrifice hunting, it is judged as the crime of blasphemy against life — banish the mastermind from Mulgore and permanently strip them of their Horde citizenship.
